converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Frank A. Pearson papers
Pearson, Frank A. (Frank Ashmore), 1887-
21-10-453
28.4 cubic feet. 28.4 cubic feet
1923-1965. Correspondence, publications, manuscripts, and photographs. Professional correspondence, manuscripts by Pearson and Warren; notes; speeches and publications; lecture notes on prices; research material on cream; statistics; notes and drafts and related correspondence on world hunger, the price of gold, and foodstuffs. Also, photographs and slides of Cornell and other scenes and subjects. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 2 4 6 8 9 22 23 70 71 72 73 75 76 77 78 79 80 81 1 3 5 7 82 19 - folder 1 ======= 2 4 6 8 9 22 23 70 71 72 73 75 76 77 78 79 80 81 1 3 5 7 82 19 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2089952
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, 2008
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Prices and Statistics, New York State College of Agriculture.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Professional correspondence concerning prices, statistics, and economics; correspondence and notes on the career of George Frederick Warren; mss. by Pearson and Warren; notes on a trip to England and other subjects; speeches and publications; lecture notes on prices; research material on cream; statistics (30 v.); notes and drafts and related correspondence on world hunger, the price of gold, and foodstuffs. Also, photographs and slides of Cornell and other scenes and subjects.
RELATED MATERIALS
Includes collection 21-10-814.
Cite As:
Frank A. Pearson papers, #21-10-453. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Color slides. Photographs. Agriculture -- Economic aspects. Warren, George F. (George Frederick), 1874-1938.
